name: Operations Breach Chain - Sidecar
story: A shared operations host now delegates its internal runbook service to a companion container in the same Pod. The learner still pivots from student to backup through cron wildcard injection, but the next clue comes from a localhost-only sidecar before the final operator-to-root escalation.
objectives: Demonstrate a mono-Pod sidecar topology by chaining cron wildcard injection, a localhost-only runbook service, and sudo PATH hijacking.

steps.title: Map the archive job
steps.description: Inspect cron and identify the backup-owned archive script.
steps.question: Which script runs every minute as backup?

steps.title: Pivot to backup
steps.description: Exploit tar wildcard injection and gain the backup context.
steps.question: Which user context do you gain after the first pivot?

steps.title: Reach the sidecar
steps.description: Use the recovered token against the localhost-only runbook service.
steps.question: Which localhost port serves the runbook sidecar?

steps.title: Identify the operator path
steps.description: Read the runbook response and identify the privileged operator command.
steps.question: Which command is allowed with sudo for operator?

steps.title: Capture the flag
steps.description: Hijack PATH during the privileged maintenance command and capture the root flag.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?
